name: Ellis Marsalis Jr.
abstract: Ellis Louis Marsalis Jr. (November 14, 1934 – April 1, 2020) was an American jazz pianist and educator.

interviews.title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020
Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Ellis Marsalis Jr.
Take 6, with Ellis Marsalis and Marcus Roberts, Piano
Take 6, with Ellis Marsalis and Marcus Roberts, Piano
Music of African-American Composers Series I

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Marsalis' and clarifies '[Winton, son of Ellis Marsalis, pianist]'. The interviewee (Sam Jones) does not respond to this specific mention with any personal anecdote or confirmation of a relationship, only acknowledging the interviewer's point about talent.

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I played a gig at some high school with Harold Battiste on the, whatever he was playin' that night, or one of the readers, and Ellis on bass and me on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Neal Caine
about_quotes.evidence: One of my great experiences was playing with Ellis Marsalis when I was in New Orleans and starting out, because I used to do his trio gig every week at Snug
about_quotes.doc_title: Neal Caine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I mean when I was in high school I was performing with Ellis Marsalis at a club... called Lu and Charlie’s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: And the period that we were putting this music together was also after, I mean, in my late high school years, say like 58, 59, going into 60, that was a very, there were some very vibrant things happening with Nat Parallot and Ellis and all those persons.

about_quotes.speaker: Rick Margitza
about_quotes.evidence: Ellis was very cool. You know once again I was just this young little white guy in New Orleans not knowing that many people so I was kind of in awe of him... but he was a sweetheart
about_quotes.doc_title: Rick Margitza interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 22, 2024
